text.title: MCP Server Development Guide Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: One MCP server exposes 53 tools across 4 unrelated domains. Tool-selection accuracy < 50%.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated MCP server as a project namespace, not a focused tool surface.
antipattern.fix: Split into 4 servers, each ≤ 20 tools, single domain.

antipattern.symptom: Tool accepts a free-text `query` field. Agent passes malformed payloads; server crashes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped Zod / Pydantic schema 'for speed'.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory schema. Without it, server rejects registration at startup.

antipattern.symptom: API key committed in source. Gitleaks fires post-publish; key rotated in panic.
antipattern.root-cause: Author passed secret as a constant 'temporarily'.
antipattern.fix: Env vars only. CI gitleaks scan blocks the publish PR.

antipattern.symptom: Published server fails on first install because tool schema field is `inputSchema` not `schema`.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory mcp-inspector smoke test in CI before publish.
